title: Polynomial Representation Using Linked Lists
description: A visual comparison of storing a polynomial as a linked list versus an array, highlighting the efficiency for sparse polynomials.
elements: Linked List Representation (Nodes containing Coefficient, Exponent, Next pointer)
relationships: The Polynomial is the abstract mathematical expression. The Array Representation stores coefficients in contiguous memory, using their index to imply the exponent, which is inefficient for sparse polynomials. The Linked List Representation stores only non-zero terms as nodes, each explicitly holding the coefficient and exponent, and points to the next term, saving memory for sparse data.
